vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on shadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/shadow, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/shadow

triage_decision.reason: Changing /etc/shadow permissions is a standard STIG remediation that follows typical hardening patterns. The fix is a simple chmod operation, unlikely to cause system breakage or lockout if applied correctly.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Potential authentication lockout if permissions are set incorrectly; Impact on services that read /etc/shadow
